title: AP Facing Severe Heat Wave, With Several Areas Register High Temperatures - Deccan Chronicle
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
20 districts have recorded more than 41 degrees Celsius, causing discomfort to people
Vijayawada: Several parts of Andhra Pradesh registered high temperatures on Friday, with Piduguralla in Palnadu district registering the highest temperature of 45.9 degrees Celsius. 252 mandals in 20 districts have recorded more than 41 degrees Celsius, causing discomfort to people.
The areas that registered high temperatures include Gudur in Nellore district recording a temperature of 45.5 degrees Celsius, followed by Peddaparupudi in Krishna district and Thorrigudipadu in NTR district registering 45.1 degrees, Addanki in Prakasam district 44.6 degrees, Peddacherlopalli in Markapuram district 44.5 degrees, Venkatagiri in Tirupati district 44.1 degrees, Karamchedu in Bapatla district 44 degrees, Sanjamala in Nandyal district and Koduru in YSR Kadapa district 43.2 degrees, Kunavaram in Polavaram district 42.6 degrees, and Thavanampalle in Chittoor district and Munagala in Visakhapatnam district reaching a temperature of 42.4 degrees Celsius.
In a statement here on Friday, AP State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) managing director Prakhar Jain said 64 mandals will witness severe heat wave conditions on Saturday. They include eight mandals in Vizianagaram, 10 in Manyam, two in Polavaram, one each in NTR and Guntur, 19 in Palnadu, six in Markapuram, 10 in Prakasam, three in Nellore and four in Tirupati.
Prakhar Jain said some of the areas may also witness rains accompanied by lightning.
As per APSDMA’s heatwave forecast, out of a total of 688 mandals, 11 witnessed severe heat waves, 69 suffered heat waves while 608 mandals reported no heat wave.
As per the forecast for Saturday, 64 mandals are to witness a heat wave, and 624 mandals will register no heat wave. For Sunday, only 21 mandals will see a heat wave while 667 mandals will not experience any heat wave.
Severe heat wave implies the maximum temperature departing from the normal by 6.4 degrees Celsius and reaching about 47 degrees Celsius. Heat waves are said to prevail when the temperature reaches nearly 45 degrees Celsius.
IMD’s Meteorological Centre, Amaravati, in its weather forecast, said Thursday’s trough / wind discontinuity from South Odisha to the Gulf of Mannar across Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu is persisting and is now seen up to 1.5 km above mean sea level.
Accordingly, IMD has issued warnings for the next five days. On May 2, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning are likely at isolated places over coastal AP, Yanam, and Rayalaseema. Hot, humid, and uncomfortable weather conditions are likely to occur over south coastal AP and Rayalaseema. So is the case for May 3 to May 5.
